serving.description: 1 portion (approximately 350 g including broth and noodles)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of lobster floating noodle is approximately 350 g, comprising lobster pieces, egg noodles, and a generous amount of flavoured broth.

nutrient_source.title: Lobster, cooked (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #15147) combined with Noodles, egg, cooked (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #20113) and broth component estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA seafood broth analogue
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend: approximately 35% cooked lobster (USDA SR Legacy #15147), 40% cooked egg noodles (USDA SR Legacy #20113), and 25% seafood broth (FAO/INFOODS SEA analogue). The 100 g reference represents the mixed dish as served. Sodium is elevated due to soy sauce and broth seasoning typical of Zi Char preparation.

gi_source.title: Noodles (egg noodles, cooked) GI analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite lobster noodle dish. GI estimated at 40 based on egg noodles (cooked) GI of approximately 40 (low GI) per Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL values, published in Diabetes Care. The lobster and broth components contribute negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 40.25) / 100 ≈ 16.1, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 11.5 × 3.5 = 40.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce and seasoned seafood broth
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced soy sauce, as a 350 g serving provides approximately 1,470 mg sodium, representing 64% of the recommended daily limit of 2,300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from egg noodles; low GI but portion-dependent carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: The dish has a low GI of approximately 40 due to egg noodles, but a standard 350 g serving delivers approximately 40 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of 16, which individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate dietary cholesterol from lobster
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While saturated fat is relatively low at approximately 1.4 g per 350 g serving, the high sodium content and moderate cholesterol (~193 mg per serving) from lobster warrant caution for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lobster Floating Noodle (龙虾漂浮面) is a premium Singaporean Zi Char dish featuring Australian lobster served over or alongside egg noodles in a rich, savoury seafood broth. The dish is a signature offering at Marina Seafood House at Boat Quay, Singapore, and is representative of the upscale Zi Char dining tradition where fresh crustaceans are cooked to order. The lobster is typically halved and simmered or briefly stir-fried before being placed atop a nest of springy egg noodles that float in a ginger-and-soy-seasoned broth. The texture contrasts firm, sweet lobster flesh with soft, slippery noodles, and the broth carries umami depth from the shellfish cooking liquor. Nutritionally, the dish provides a meaningful protein contribution (~32 g per 350 g serving) from the lobster, with moderate carbohydrate from the noodles and a low glycemic index of approximately 40. Sodium content is high due to soy sauce and seasoned broth, making it a concern for individuals with hypertension. The dish is not suitable for those with crustacean, gluten, egg, or soy allergies. It is not halal-certified in most Zi Char settings. Clinically, the low GI and high protein content are positive attributes, but the sodium load and moderate cholesterol content require portion awareness for individuals managing cardiovascular disease or hypertension.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked lobster and cooked egg noodles, supplemented by FAO/INFOODS SEA broth analogue data. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (egg noodles, cooked) per the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level sources are high-quality approved sources, but the composite estimation introduces uncertainty.
notes: 1. The food name in the input record contains a typographical error ('Austrarian' instead of 'Australian'); the canonical name has been corrected to 'Lobster Noodle Soup (Floating Noodle Style)' and the original misspelling is preserved as an alias for retrieval purposes. 2. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend: ~35% cooked lobster (USDA SR Legacy #15147), ~40% cooked egg noodles (USDA SR Legacy #20113), ~25% seafood broth analogue (FAO/INFOODS SEA). 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source databases used for this estimation (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #15147 for cooked lobster and SR Legacy #20113 for cooked egg noodles, and FAO/INFOODS SEA seafood broth analogue); vitamin D data is absent from all three component sources and therefore cannot be derived for this composite dish. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source databases used for this estimation (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #15147 for cooked lobster and SR Legacy #20113 for cooked egg noodles, and FAO/INFOODS SEA seafood broth analogue); vitamin E data is absent from all three component sources and therefore cannot be derived for this composite dish. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed zero as lobster and egg noodles contain no industrially produced trans fats; value is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component data. 6. GI is an analogous estimate based on egg noodles (cooked) GI ≈ 40 per Atkinson et al. 2008; gi_category is 'low' (GI ≤ 55). 7. Halal status is listed as false as Zi Char restaurants in Singapore typically are not halal-certified; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant. 8. The dish is served at Marina Seafood House, Boat Quay, Singapore, at SGD 22.80 per 100 g portion as listed on field survey data.
